title: Dangling symlink traversal failure when using bud with layer cache
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**
Image build fails when building an image using the layer cache from a Dockerfile while adding a symlink that will be dangling at build time due to the target not being located in the context directory.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1.cat << EOF >Dockerfile
FROM fedora
COPY run-mysqld /tmp/
EOF
2. ln -s /bin/run-mysqld run-mysqld
3. buildah bud --layers .
4. buildah bud --layers .

**Describe the results you received:**
Second `buildah bud` fails with:
```
error checking if cached image exists from a previous build: error checking if copied files match: error resolving symlinks and comparing modified times: "/tmp/reproducer/run-mysqld": error checking if modified time of resolved symbolic link is greater: error walking file tree "/tmp/reproducer/run-mysqld": error evaluating symlink "run-mysqld": lstat /bin: no such file or directory
: exit status 1
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Buildah sees that the symlink is dangling and does not try to check the target

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

buildah-1.5-2.gite94b4f9.fc28.x86_64

**Output of `buildah version`:**

Version:         1.5
Go Version:      go1.10.5
Image Spec:      1.0.0
Runtime Spec:    1.0.0
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64

comments.body: @umohnani8 might this have been cleaned up with your latest changes to the layer code?

@TomSweeneyRedHat no it wouldn't. It is failing because bud expects the symlink to exist so it can compare the timestamps to know whether to use from cache or not. Didn't think of a dangling symlink case. I guess the fix would be to ignore it if it is dangling and run the build process again i.e not use cached layers? Not sure what docker does.

@rhatdan @umohnani8 It does, although I do not know what happens inside.

The docker reproducer is a little different as it fails when only the symlink is copied in so I have put the symlink into a directory and copy in the whole dir content instead (which is closer to my actual use case anyway):

```
$ cat << EOF >Dockerfile
FROM fedora
COPY test /tmp/
EOF
$ mkdir test; ln -s /bin/run-mysqld test/run-mysqld
$ docker-binary build .
Sending build context to Docker daemon 3.072 kB
Step 1/2 : FROM fedora
 ---> 8c568f104326
Step 2/2 : COPY test /tmp/
 ---> f161571d5986
Removing intermediate container ac9572bed681
Successfully built f161571d5986
$ docker-binary build .
Sending build context to Docker daemon 3.072 kB
Step 1/2 : FROM fedora
 ---> 8c568f104326
Step 2/2 : COPY test /tmp/
 ---> Using cache
 ---> f161571d5986
Successfully built f161571d5986
$ docker-binary run --rm -ti f161571d5986 bash -c "ls -al /tmp"
total 16
drwxrwxrwt. 1 root root 4096 Dec  6 06:21 .
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 4096 Dec  6 06:23 ..
-rwx------. 1 root root 1292 Nov  6 06:48 ks-script-xc5fzv4d
lrwxrwxrwx. 1 root root   15 Dec  6 06:20 run-mysqld -> /bin/run-mysqld
```

This still fails when using buildah against the same sources:

```
$ buildah bud  --layers .
STEP 1: FROM fedora
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:d0483bd5a55488f5ba6383a5cc8553d5101864f03acd07eabc5df7563c3692cf
 83.23 MiB / 83.23 MiB [====================================================] 3s
Copying config sha256:8c568f1043264e34f0a8774587266565c7e5e54e9ea6b97ab459086d18ac5175
 2.29 KiB / 2.29 KiB [======================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: COPY test /tmp/
--> 152240725baccae54ea4a6abd8fe2d00d3375648d74767c6176d1e91d3acfbd5
STEP 3: COMMIT 
$ buildah bud  --layers .
STEP 1: FROM fedora
STEP 2: COPY test /tmp/
error checking if cached image exists from a previous build: error checking if copied files match: error resolving symlinks and comparing modified times: "/tmp/testing/test": error checking if modified time of resolved symbolic link is greater: error walking file tree "/tmp/testing/test": error evaluating symlink "test/run-mysqld": lstat /bin: no such file or directory
: exit status 
```
@umohnani8 @TomSweeneyRedHat @pkubatrh Is this still an issue or has it been fixed?

@rhatdan Still seeing it in latest master

```
$ git rev-parse HEAD
36605c29eb258caa9720dbd42622269a81dd1bfa
$ cat << EOF >Dockerfile
FROM fedora
COPY test /tmp/
EOF
$ mkdir test; ln -s /bin/run-mysqld test/run-mysqld
$ ./buildah bud --layers .; ./buildah bud --layers .
...
STEP 1: FROM fedora
STEP 2: COPY test /tmp/
error checking if cached image exists from a previous build: error checking if copied files match: error resolving symlinks and comparing modified times: "/home/pkubat/go/buildah/src/github.com/containers/buildah/test": error checking if modified time of resolved symbolic link is greater: error walking file tree "/home/pkubat/go/buildah/src/github.com/containers/buildah/test": error evaluating symlink "test/run-mysqld": lstat /bin: no such file or directory
: exit status 1
```
@TomSweeneyRedHat is this the code you were working on for handling symlinks?
Yes and I need to revisit.
